StatusNote: The 2016-17 swimming archive now includes the October 15, 2016 SCISA State Championship results from the official meet PDF shared by the user.
RecapParagraphs: The recovered 2016-17 SCISA season adds another fall-era state meet for St. Andrew's. At the October 15, 2016 SCISA State Championship, the girls finished third in the AA-A standings with 101 points behind Grace Van Brunt's two individual state titles and three top-two relay finishes.

Grace Van Brunt won the girls 200 IM in 2:12.44 and the 100 backstroke in 1:00.80, while Gaby Van Brunt placed second in both the 200 freestyle and 100 butterfly. The girls also finished second in the 200 medley relay and 400 freestyle relay, and added points from the 200 freestyle relay and Corinne Standing's 100 breaststroke.

The boys finished 11th in the AA-A standings with 25 points. Jack Fox-Marrs placed sixth in the 50 freestyle and fourth in the 100 freestyle, and the boys scored in both the 200 medley and 200 freestyle relays.
HighlightNotes: Grace Van Brunt won SCISA AA-A state titles in the girls 200 IM and 100 backstroke.
Gaby Van Brunt finished 2nd in both the girls 200 freestyle and 100 butterfly.
The girls team finished 3rd in the SCISA AA-A standings with 101 points.
The boys team finished 11th in the SCISA AA-A standings with 25 points.

StatusNote: The 2017-18 swimming archive now includes the fully loaded October 14, 2017 SCISA State Championship at the Augusta Aquatic Center in Augusta, Georgia, using SwimMeet.com's archived St. Andrew's school-summary, event, and team-score pages.
RecapParagraphs: The recovered 2017-18 SCISA archive adds an earlier championship season for St. Andrew's. The varsity girls finished fourth in the SCISA 2A standings with 91 points behind two individual state titles and two scoring relays at the October 14, 2017 state meet in Augusta.

Gaby Vanbrunt won the girls 50 freestyle in 25.10 and added a runner-up finish in the 100 butterfly, while Grace Vanbrunt won the girls 100 backstroke and took third in the 50 freestyle. The girls 200 medley relay finished third and the 400 freestyle relay finished second to round out the scoring core.

The boys side finished 11th in SCISA 2A with 30 points. Jack Fox-Marrs placed fifth in both the 200 freestyle and 100 freestyle, Jonathan Young finished ninth in the 50 freestyle, and the boys freestyle and medley relays added top-nine finishes.
HighlightNotes: Gaby Vanbrunt won the girls SCISA 2A 50 freestyle state title in 25.10.
Grace Vanbrunt won the girls SCISA 2A 100 backstroke state title in 1:01.46.
The girls varsity team finished 4th in the SCISA 2A standings with 91 points.
The boys varsity team finished 11th in the SCISA 2A standings with 30 points.

StatusNote: The 2018-19 swimming archive begins with the fully loaded January 26, 2019 GISA State Championship in Atlanta, including both varsity and middle school St. Andrew's results from the official meet PDF.
StateMeetLocation: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
RecapParagraphs: The St. Andrew's swim archive now reaches back to January 26, 2019, when Gaby Van Brunt powered the varsity girls to a fourth-place finish at the GISA State Championship in Atlanta. Van Brunt won the 50 freestyle in a record 23.75, the 100 freestyle in 52.40, and the 100 backstroke in a record 58.20.

The girls also scored in both varsity relays. Marie Audebert, Libby Peaster, Van Brunt, and Molly Fox-Marrs placed fifth in the 200 medley relay, and Fox-Marrs, Audebert, Sarah Rowland, and Van Brunt matched that fifth-place finish in the 200 freestyle relay to help St. Andrew's total 43 points.

The boys side added a seventh-place varsity 200 medley relay from Tyler Awad, Sumter Archer, Noah Linfoot, and Matt Eling, while middle school swimmers Luke Peaster and Ian Given carried the younger St. Andrew's entries across sprint, backstroke, and breaststroke events.
HighlightNotes: Gaby Van Brunt won three girls varsity state titles in the 50 freestyle, 100 freestyle, and 100 backstroke.
Van Brunt set GISA records in the girls 50 freestyle at 23.75 and 100 backstroke at 58.20 on January 26, 2019.
The girls varsity team finished 4th in the GISA standings with 43 points.
The boys varsity 200 medley relay finished 7th to score the program's four boys team points.

StatusNote: The 2019-20 swimming archive begins with the fully loaded January 25, 2020 GISA State Championship in Atlanta, including both varsity and middle school St. Andrew's results from the official meet PDF.
StateMeetLocation: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
RecapParagraphs: Gaby Van Brunt again led the St. Andrew's girls at the January 25, 2020 GISA State Championship in Atlanta. Van Brunt finished second in the 50 freestyle, then won the 100 butterfly in 57.05 and the 100 freestyle in 54.06 as the Lions climbed to third in the girls varsity standings with 47 points.

Addy Sy supplied more varsity scoring by placing seventh in the 200 IM and fifth in the 100 backstroke, and the girls scored in both relays. Marie Audebert, Sy, Van Brunt, and Molly Fox-Marrs finished fifth in the 200 medley relay, while Sy, Fox-Marrs, Sarah Rowland, and Van Brunt took fifth in the 200 freestyle relay.

The middle school archive adds Sierra Abbasi's girls entries and Ian Given's boys entries, including Given's eighth-place finish in the 50 breaststroke that put St. Andrew's 15th in the boys middle school team standings.
HighlightNotes: Gaby Van Brunt won girls varsity state titles in the 100 butterfly and 100 freestyle.
The girls varsity team finished 3rd in the GISA standings with 47 points.
Addy Sy scored in both the girls 200 IM and 100 backstroke.
Ian Given placed 8th in the boys middle school 50 breaststroke for the program's only middle school team point.

StatusNote: The 2021-22 swimming archive begins with the fully loaded January 29, 2022 GISA State Championship at the McAuley Aquatic Center at Georgia Tech in Atlanta.
StateMeetLocation: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
RecapParagraphs: Gaby Van Brunt produced another title sweep for St. Andrew's at the January 29, 2022 GISA State Championship at Georgia Tech. She won the girls 50 freestyle in a record 22.99, the 100 butterfly in a record 54.13, and the 100 backstroke in another record 54.17.

Those three wins accounted for all 27 girls points and pushed St. Andrew's to sixth in the varsity girls standings. On the boys side, Caden Cahill finished third in the 200 freestyle, third in the 100 butterfly, and third in the 100 backstroke, while Ian Given added seventh-place points in the 200 IM to lift the boys to seventh with 20 points.

The middle school side in 2022 preserves a smaller but useful St. Andrew's group: Sydney Linfoot swam the girls 50 freestyle, Austin Waters and Robert Federal handled sprint and breaststroke entries, and Eli Rall covered freestyle, butterfly, and backstroke events.
HighlightNotes: Gaby Van Brunt won three girls varsity state titles in the 50 freestyle, 100 butterfly, and 100 backstroke.
Van Brunt set GISA records at 22.99 in the girls 50 freestyle, 54.13 in the girls 100 butterfly, and 54.17 in the girls 100 backstroke.
Caden Cahill placed 3rd in the boys 200 freestyle, 100 butterfly, and 100 backstroke.
The girls varsity team finished 6th with 27 points, and the boys varsity team finished 7th with 20 points.

StatusNote: The 2022-23 swimming archive begins with the fully loaded February 2, 2023 GIAA State Championship in Atlanta, including both varsity and middle school St. Andrew's results from the official meet PDF.
RecapParagraphs: The St. Andrew's swim archive now reaches back to February 2, 2023, when the Lions competed in the GIAA State Championship in Atlanta. The varsity girls side delivered the defining moment of the season through Gaby Van Brunt, who won the 100 butterfly, 100 freestyle, and 100 backstroke, including a state-record 53.03 in the butterfly.

Those three wins accounted for all 27 varsity girls points and pushed St. Andrew's to seventh in the girls standings. Ian Given supplied the entire boys varsity scoring by placing seventh in the 100 freestyle, fourth in the 100 backstroke, and seventh in the 100 breaststroke, helping the boys finish ninth with nine points and lifting the combined varsity team to ninth overall with 36 points.

The middle school side adds meaningful program depth to the archive. Tristan Tsang scored in both the 50 and 100 freestyle, the boys 200 freestyle relay finished ninth, the boys 200 medley relay also completed the meet, and several additional St. Andrew's swims, disqualifications, and no-starts now preserve a fuller picture of the 2022-23 roster.
HighlightNotes: Gaby Van Brunt won three girls varsity state titles in the 100 butterfly, 100 freestyle, and 100 backstroke.
Van Brunt set the GIAA girls 100 butterfly record at 53.03 on February 2, 2023.
Ian Given scored in three boys varsity events and helped the boys finish 9th with nine points.
Tristan Tsang placed 3rd in the boys middle school 100 freestyle and 4th in the 50 freestyle.

StatusNote: The 2023-24 swimming archive begins with the fully loaded January 27, 2024 GIAA State Championship at the McAuley Aquatic Center at Georgia Tech in Atlanta.
StateMeetLocation: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
RecapParagraphs: The St. Andrew's swimming archive opens with a strong first varsity snapshot from the January 27, 2024 GIAA State Championship at Georgia Tech. Jossy Thompson supplied the headline results by sweeping three individual girls state titles in the 50 freestyle, 100 butterfly, and 100 breaststroke.

Those three wins accounted for every St. Andrew's girls point and lifted the Lions to seventh in the varsity team standings with 27 points. Thompson's opening archive day already gives the program a clear championship anchor across sprint freestyle, butterfly, and breaststroke.

The boys side added real team depth around Ian Given and Tristan Tsang. Given placed third in the 200 IM, fourth in the 100 backstroke, and seventh in the 100 freestyle, Tsang added a fifth-place finish in the 500 freestyle and an eighth-place finish in the 100 butterfly, and the boys medley and freestyle relays both scored to push St. Andrew's to sixth in the varsity standings with 28 points.
HighlightNotes: Jossy Thompson won three girls varsity state titles in the 50 freestyle, 100 butterfly, and 100 breaststroke.
The girls team finished 7th in the GIAA varsity standings with 27 points.
Ian Given placed 3rd in the boys 200 IM and 4th in the boys 100 backstroke.
The boys team finished 6th in the GIAA varsity standings with 28 points.

StatusNote: The 2024-25 swimming archive now includes the fully loaded February 1, 2025 GIAA State Championship at the McAuley Aquatic Center at Georgia Tech in Atlanta, including both varsity and middle school St. Andrew's results from the official meet PDF.
StateMeetLocation: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
RecapParagraphs: The 2024-25 state meet centered on Jossy Thompson again for St. Andrew's. Thompson finished second in the girls 50 freestyle in 24.27, added another runner-up finish in the 100 butterfly at 58.28, and then won the girls 100 breaststroke in 1:06.65 to lift the varsity girls to fifth in the GIAA standings with 23 points.

The boys varsity side scored entirely through Tristan Tsang. Tsang placed fourth in the 200 IM, third in the 100 butterfly, and fifth in the 100 breaststroke to account for all 15 Lions points and a 10th-place team finish, while Ian Willis and James Crake added sprint and backstroke depth.

The middle-school girls added the broader team result by finishing third with 40 points. St. Andrew's took third in both middle-school girls relays, got individual scoring swims from Mabyl Jefferson, Hazel Jefferson, and Anna Neufeld, and rounded out the archive with a full boys middle-school relay and stroke slate from Miles Glienke, Zac Berwitz, Nouh Siddiqi, and Hank Tucker.
HighlightNotes: Jossy Thompson won the girls varsity 100 breaststroke state title and finished 2nd in both the 50 freestyle and 100 butterfly.
The varsity girls team finished 5th in the GIAA standings with 23 points.
Tristan Tsang scored in all three of his varsity boys events and led the Lions to 10th place with 15 points.
The middle-school girls team finished 3rd in the GIAA standings with 40 points behind two third-place relays and scoring swims from Mabyl Jefferson, Hazel Jefferson, and Anna Neufeld.

StatusNote: The 2025-26 swimming archive now includes the fully loaded February 9, 2026 GIAA State Championship at the McAuley Aquatic Center at Georgia Tech in Atlanta, including both varsity and middle school St. Andrew's results from the official meet PDF.
StateMeetLocation: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
RecapParagraphs: The 2025-26 state meet gave St. Andrew's another headline day from Jossy Thompson. Thompson won the girls 50 freestyle in 24.25, added a second varsity title in the 100 butterfly at 58.68, and finished second in the 100 breaststroke, while Mabyl Jefferson added three scoring swims to push the varsity girls to fifth in the GIAA standings with 36 points.

The boys side scored through Tristan Tsang and the 200 freestyle relay. Tsang placed third in both the 200 freestyle and 100 breaststroke while adding a sixth-place finish in the 100 butterfly, and Oliver Kovel, Tsang, Ian Willis, and Reefe Reiter took fourth in the varsity 200 freestyle relay to help the Lions tie for eighth with 25 points.

The middle-school girls added the deepest team result of the season by finishing second with 46 points. Anna Neufeld and Hazel Jefferson scored across the IM, freestyle, butterfly, and backstroke events, and St. Andrew's finished runner-up in both the 200 medley and 200 freestyle relays while the boys side added a full relay and stroke slate to round out the archive.
HighlightNotes: Jossy Thompson won girls varsity state titles in the 50 freestyle and 100 butterfly, then finished 2nd in the 100 breaststroke.
The varsity girls team finished 5th in the GIAA standings with 36 points.
Tristan Tsang placed 3rd in both the boys 200 freestyle and 100 breaststroke and helped the boys varsity 200 freestyle relay finish 4th.
The middle-school girls team finished 2nd in the GIAA standings with 46 points behind two runner-up relays and scoring swims from Anna Neufeld and Hazel Jefferson.
